topic: Why being 'average' is your biggest financial asset
hook_summary: Stop trying to be special. Average is the cheat code.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone says you need to be exceptional to make real money. I think that's a trap.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up of @daniel sitting at a rustic wooden table in a sunlit coffee shop, natural light from a large window on his left, candid documentary style, locked-off camera.

scenes.audio_text: The most financially secure people I know? They're not geniuses. They're average people who showed up every day for a decade.
scenes.visual_prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot of @daniel walking down a quiet tree-lined street in a residential neighborhood, afternoon golden hour light, motion follows him slowly.

scenes.audio_text: I spent years trying to be the smartest guy in the room. Got nowhere. Then I just picked a boring skill and ground it out.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot of @daniel leaning against a brick wall in an urban alley, moody overcast light, slightly grainy film look, slow push-in.

scenes.audio_text: Funny thing—once I stopped chasing unique, the money showed up. Consistency beats talent when talent isn't consistent.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium shot of @daniel sitting on a park bench under a large oak tree, dappled sunlight, candid looking off-camera, locked-off.

scenes.audio_text: So stop trying to be special. Just be average, but do it longer than anyone else.
scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of @daniel in his kitchen, leaning on a counter, warm tungsten light, direct eye contact with camera, clean flat landing.

loop_back_line: Average is the new unfair advantage.
